title: Muhammad Al Hasib
text:
Hello! I am Hasib, a PhD student in Public Affairs at The University of Texas at Dallas. My work sits at the intersection of technology and governance, specifically focusing on how AI impacts public service delivery.
This website showcases my coursework, research, and data analysis projects.

title: Muhammad Al Hasib

title: Muhammad Al Hasib
text:
Ongoing Research






NoteAI & Public Values





AI adoption in public services: Testing trade-offs and Balancing Administrative and Democratic Values. This project examines the tension between efficiency and accountability.









NoteEthical Climate





The Mediating Role of Person-Organization Fit: Evidence from the Caribbean public sector regarding ethical climate and organizational commitment.









NoteEducation Policy





Impacts of Education Policy across Community Types: An Interrupted Time Series (ITS) analysis of Indiana’s Voluntary Open Enrollment using NAEP data.

title: About Me
text:
I am a PhD student in Public and Nonprofit Management, driven by a deep interest in research, policy analysis, and public management. My academic journey has been shaped by a passion for understanding complex governance systems, the impact of public policy, and the relationship between institutions and the people they serve.
Currently, I work as a Teaching Assistant and actively contribute to the research areas of education policy, social equity, behavioral public administration, and social welfare. I am particularly interested in using evidence-based approaches to evaluate programs and design inclusive, effective policies.
My professional experience spans from BRAC University and Stanford University to Oxfam GB. Also, my experience encompasses academic writing, data analysis, and cross-cultural collaboration. I’m also committed to mentoring students and contributing to an engaged educational environment.
I welcome connections and conversations around research collaboration, program and policy development, and consulting opportunities.

title: EPPS 6323 Assignments
text: Below are my assignments for EPPS 6323 – Knowledge Mining, showcasing various data science and machine learning applications in public policy.

title: EPPS 6323 Assignments
text:
Assignment List

Assignment 01: Introduction to R & Quarto

Initial setup and exploratory data analysis (EDA).

Assignment 02: Linear Regression & Model Selection

Predicting outcomes using subset selection and regularization techniques.

Assignment 03: Classification Methods

Implementing Logistic Regression, LDA, and QDA on public sector datasets.

Assignment 04: Resampling & Cross-Validation

Estimating test error using Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV) and k-fold CV.
